answers: Common cooking herbs: basil, parsley, cilantro, rosemary, thyme, mint, oregano, dill, chives, sage.
Fresh vs dried: fresh has brighter flavor, add at end. Dried is more concentrated — use 1/3 of fresh amount, add early.
Grow herbs at home: basil, mint, and cilantro grow easily in pots on a windowsill. Need 6+ hours of sunlight.
Herb tip: freeze fresh herbs in olive oil in ice cube trays — perfect flavor bombs for cooking later.
